zotero.abstractNote: Recurrent Gaussian Process (RGP) models are a powerful class of models for time-series data. However, exact inference is intractable, and current approximate inference methods rely on mean-field assumptions that ignore the temporal correlations in the latent state. We propose a new variational inference framework for RGPs that allows for non-mean-field posterior approximations. We achieve this by using a structured variational distribution that captures the temporal dependencies in the latent state. We demonstrate that our approach leads to better predictive performance and more accurate uncertainty estimates on a range of time-series datasets.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 36th International Conference on Machine Learning
